services.title: Services
services.subtitle: Therapeutic treatments thoughtfully tailored to your wellbeing

servicesPage.title: Our Services
servicesPage.subtitle: Expert massage therapy tailored to your unique needs, offered in a calm and welcoming setting in Gatineau, QC.

therapeutic.longDescription: A result-driven treatment that draws on a range of manual therapy techniques — including myofascial release, trigger point therapy, and neuromuscular work — to address specific musculoskeletal conditions, postural imbalances, and recovery goals. Each session begins with an assessment of your current concerns and is structured around a clear therapeutic objective. Pressure and technique are continuously adapted throughout the session.
therapeutic.whoFor: Anyone managing a specific condition such as chronic back pain, neck stiffness, sciatica, tendinitis, or postural dysfunction — as well as those recovering from injury or seeking a clinically-focused approach.

deepTissue.longDescription: A targeted, pressure-focused technique that works through superficial muscle layers to reach the underlying connective tissue and fascia. Exceptionally effective for chronic pain, postural issues, and sports recovery. Pressure is always adjusted to your comfort level and therapeutic goals.
deepTissue.whoFor: Athletes, people with chronic pain or tension, those recovering from injury, or anyone who carries stress in specific muscle groups.

relaxation.longDescription: A slow, intentional treatment designed to activate the parasympathetic nervous system, quiet mental chatter, and bring the whole body into a state of deep, unhurried calm. Lighter in pressure than therapeutic or deep tissue work, with a focus on the quality of presence and the experience of stillness.
relaxation.whoFor: Anyone experiencing burnout, anxiety, insomnia, or simply in need of a genuine pause from the demands of daily life.

lymphatic.longDescription: Manual Lymphatic Drainage uses light, precise, rhythmic strokes to activate the superficial lymphatic vessels and encourage the movement of lymph fluid throughout the body. Particularly effective post-surgery, post-injury, or when the body is under immune stress, this gentle technique works with the body's own drainage system rather than against it. Sessions are always calm, meditative in pace, and deeply restorative.
lymphatic.whoFor: Those recovering from surgery or injury, people experiencing edema or chronic puffiness, individuals with compromised immune function, or anyone seeking a gentle, deeply restorative treatment.

children.longDescription: Gentle, playful, and always age-appropriate — children's sessions use carefully adapted techniques to suit young bodies, from newborns through to teens. Each session is designed to be comfortable, safe, and even enjoyable, supporting healthy development at every stage of growth.
children.whoFor: Babies (as young as a few months old), toddlers, children, and teenagers.

couples.longDescription: Two people, one shared experience. In a couples massage, both partners receive their own full treatment simultaneously — each session independently adapted to each person's needs, tension patterns, and comfort level. Whether it's a gift, a celebration, or simply a moment of shared care, it's one of the most meaningful ways to experience massage therapy together.
couples.whoFor: Couples, close friends, or family members who want to enjoy a massage experience together. Also a thoughtful gift for anniversaries, birthdays, Valentine's Day, or any special occasion.

preview.title: Meet Olha Shelest
preview.subtitle: Professional Massage Therapist
preview.bio: With 10+ years of experience and a deep passion for holistic healing, Olha brings both expertise and genuine care to every treatment — helping you move, feel, and live better.

page.title: About Olha Shelest
page.subtitle: Professional Massage Therapist · Gatineau, QC
page.bio1: Olha Shelest is a Professional Massage Therapist based in Gatineau, Quebec. With over 10 years of experience, a deep passion for holistic healing, and an unwavering commitment to excellence, Olha brings both expertise and genuine care to every treatment.
page.bio2: Trained in a comprehensive range of therapeutic techniques, Olha specializes in helping clients overcome pain, recover from injury, manage stress, and achieve lasting relaxation. Her approach is always personalized — every session is thoughtfully tailored to your unique needs and health goals.
page.bio3: Olha believes that massage therapy is far more than a luxury. It is an essential component of a balanced, healthy lifestyle. Whether you are an athlete seeking faster recovery, someone managing chronic pain, or simply in need of deep relaxation, Olha is here to guide you on your path to wellness.
page.bio4: She welcomes clients of all backgrounds and adapts her techniques to suit each individual's comfort level and therapeutic objectives. Every visit is a step toward a healthier, more balanced you.
page.bioCta: Explore our <servicesLink>massage services</servicesLink> to find the right treatment for you, or <contactLink>get in touch</contactLink> to book your first session.
page.profileAlt: Olha Shelest — Registered Massage Therapist in Gatineau
page.amqText: Olha is a verified member of the Association des Massothérapeutes du Québec (AMQ), ensuring her practice meets the highest professional and ethical standards in Quebec.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle: How we collect, use, and protect your personal information

sections.title: 1. Information We Collect
sections.content: We collect personal information that you voluntarily provide when you contact us through our website, including your name, email address, phone number, and the content of your message. We may also collect certain technical information automatically, such as your IP address and browser type, for security and analytics purposes.

sections.title: 2. How We Use Your Information
sections.content: We use the information you provide solely to respond to your inquiries, schedule appointments, and communicate with you about our services. We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer your personal information to third parties without your consent, except as required by law.

sections.title: 3. Health Information
sections.content: As part of providing massage therapy services, we may collect health-related information to ensure your safety and provide appropriate care. This information is treated with the utmost confidentiality and is protected in accordance with applicable Quebec privacy legislation.

sections.title: 4. Data Retention
sections.content: We retain your personal information only for as long as necessary to fulfill the purposes for which it was collected, or as required by applicable laws and professional regulations. Client health records are retained in accordance with AMQ standards.

sections.title: 5. Your Rights
sections.content: Under Quebec's Act Respecting the Protection of Personal Information in the Private Sector (Law 25) and Canada's PIPEDA, you have the right to access, correct, or request deletion of your personal information. To exercise these rights, please contact us.

sections.title: 6. Security
sections.content: We implement appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect your personal information against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, or destruction. Our contact form uses HTTPS encryption to protect data in transit.

sections.title: 7. Cookies
sections.content: Our website may use essential cookies to ensure proper functionality. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies. You can control cookie settings through your browser preferences.

sections.title: 8. Contact Us
sections.content: If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy or our data practices, please contact us.

cta.title: Ready to Feel Your Best?
cta.subtitle: Take the first step toward lasting wellness. Book a session with Olha today.

items.question: Do you provide receipts for insurance?
items.answer: Yes. As an AMQ-registered massage therapist, I provide official receipts accepted by most Canadian group insurance plans and employee benefit programs. No doctor's referral is required — check your plan details for coverage amounts.

items.question: Which areas do you serve?
items.answer: My practice is located at 148 Rue Eddy in Hull–Gatineau, QC — just minutes from the Portage and Alexandra bridges. I serve clients from Gatineau, Hull, Aylmer, and Ottawa, including federal employees at Place du Portage and Terrasses de la Chaudière who are a short walk away.

items.question: What should I expect during my first massage?
items.answer: We start with a short intake conversation to understand your goals, health history, and any areas of concern. Throughout the session you remain covered by a sheet — the therapist works on one area at a time, keeping everything else draped. First sessions typically run the full booked time, with follow-up care recommendations at the end.

items.question: How often should I get a massage?
items.answer: It depends on your goal. For acute pain or injury recovery, weekly sessions are often recommended initially. For stress management or general wellness, monthly sessions maintain the benefits well. We will discuss the right cadence for you during your first visit.

items.question: Do you offer massage for children?
items.answer: Yes. Children's massage is a gentle, therapeutic service available with parental consent and presence. It can help with growing pains, stress, sleep difficulties, and general relaxation. Sessions are adapted in duration and technique to each child's needs.

items.question: What is the difference between therapeutic and relaxation massage?
items.answer: Therapeutic massage targets specific pain, tension, or dysfunction — it is goal-oriented and may include deeper pressure and focused work on problem areas. Relaxation massage uses a flowing, lighter touch over the whole body to reduce stress and promote calm. If you are unsure which you need, describe your main concern during booking and we will choose together.

items.question: Is deep tissue massage painful?
items.answer: Deep tissue work involves more pressure than relaxation massage, but it should never feel unbearable. I communicate with you throughout to stay within your comfort zone. Some clients feel mild soreness for 24–48 hours after — similar to post-workout soreness — which is normal and resolves quickly.

items.question: Who can benefit from lymphatic drainage massage?
items.answer: Lymphatic drainage is particularly beneficial after surgery or injury (once cleared by your doctor), for persistent swelling or water retention, chronic fatigue, and immune support. It uses very light, rhythmic strokes — quite different from deep tissue work. It is contraindicated for active infections, blood clots, or certain heart and kidney conditions.

items.question: Can two people book a couples massage session together?
items.answer: Yes — couples massage is available. Two people are treated simultaneously in the same room. It is a popular choice for partners, friends, or family members who want to share the experience. Each person can choose a different massage type.

items.question: Can massage help with chronic pain or injury recovery?
items.answer: Yes. Therapeutic and deep tissue massage are effective tools for managing chronic pain, muscle tension, and post-injury recovery. Regular sessions can reduce inflammation, improve circulation, and restore range of motion. Massage also works well alongside physiotherapy or other treatments — the technique and pressure are always adapted to your specific condition.

hull.title: Massage in Hull–Gatineau
hull.subtitle: Professional massage therapy in the heart of Hull, steps from Place du Portage — serving Hull residents and Ottawa federal workers.
paragraphs: Located at 148 Rue Eddy in the heart of Hull, Olha Shelest's practice is at the centre of Gatineau's most connected neighbourhood. Whether you live on boulevard Saint-Joseph, rue Laval, or anywhere in Vieux-Hull, professional massage therapy is close to home — no long drive required.

Hull is a vibrant, urban community with a high concentration of federal government workers, young professionals, and families. The stresses of desk work, long commutes, and everyday life accumulate. Therapeutic massage, deep tissue work, or a relaxation session can make a measurable difference — and a practice in your own neighbourhood makes it easy to maintain a regular routine.

Olha is an AMQ-registered massage therapist offering six massage types: therapeutic massage for pain and injury, deep tissue massage for chronic tension, relaxation massage for stress, lymphatic drainage, children's massage, and couples massage. Official insurance receipts are provided for all sessions.

gettingHere.title: Getting Here
gettingHere.body: The practice is at 148 Rue Eddy Unit 2, between boulevard Saint-Laurent and rue Laurier. Free street parking is available on Rue Eddy and surrounding streets. If you work at Place du Portage or Terrasses de la Chaudière, the practice is a 3 to 5 minute walk. From downtown Ottawa, you can walk across the Portage bridge in under 10 minutes.

items.question: Where exactly is your practice located in Hull?
items.answer: The practice is at 148 Rue Eddy Unit 2 in Hull–Gatineau — between boulevard Saint-Laurent and rue Laurier, steps from Place du Portage. It is a short walk from Terrasses de la Chaudière and a 5-minute walk from the Portage bridge.

items.question: Is there parking near your Hull location?
items.answer: Yes — free street parking is available directly on Rue Eddy and on surrounding streets (rue Laurier, rue Laval). There is no paid parking lot required.

items.question: Do you serve federal employees at Place du Portage?
items.answer: Yes. Place du Portage and Terrasses de la Chaudière are a 3 to 5 minute walk from the practice. Many federal workers book sessions during lunch or after work — sessions are available Monday through Saturday.

ottawa.title: Massage Therapy Near Ottawa
ottawa.subtitle: A 5-minute walk from downtown Ottawa across the Portage bridge — massage therapy for Ottawa residents and federal workers.
paragraphs: If you work or live in Ottawa and have been looking for quality massage therapy without the downtown Ottawa prices, Olha Shelest's practice in Hull–Gatineau is closer than you think. From downtown Ottawa — Byward Market, Centretown, Sandy Hill, Lowertown — it is a short 5-minute walk across the Portage bridge or a quick drive via the Alexandra or Macdonald-Cartier bridge.

The National Capital Region's largest employer is the federal government, and many of its employees already cross the river daily. Place du Portage — just steps from the Portage bridge on the Ottawa side — connects directly to Rue Eddy on foot. Federal workers at Terrasses de la Chaudière, Les Terrasses de l'île, and Promenade du Portage are all within a 5-minute walk.
Olha is an AMQ-registered massage therapist. Insurance receipts are provided for all sessions — accepted by most federal employee benefit plans and private group insurance.

gettingHere.title: Getting Here from Ottawa
gettingHere.body: On foot: walk across the Portage bridge from downtown Ottawa (5–7 minutes from Place du Portage). By car: 5–10 minutes via the Portage, Alexandra, or Macdonald-Cartier bridge — free street parking on Rue Eddy. By transit: STO routes 31, 36, and 37 cross the river from Ottawa and stop within walking distance. Address: 148 Rue Eddy Unit 2, Hull–Gatineau, QC J8X 2W8.

items.question: How do I get to your practice from Ottawa?
items.answer: The easiest route is on foot via the Portage bridge — 5 to 7 minutes from Place du Portage, 10 minutes from Byward Market or Centretown. By car, it is 5 to 10 minutes via the Portage, Alexandra, or Macdonald-Cartier bridge. STO bus routes 31, 36, and 37 cross the river and stop within walking distance.

items.question: Is your practice walkable from downtown Ottawa without a car?
items.answer: Yes. If you work at Place du Portage or anywhere along the Portage bridge corridor, the practice is a 5 to 7 minute walk across the bridge. From Byward Market or Rideau Centre, it is about 10 to 12 minutes on foot. Free parking is available on Rue Eddy for those who prefer to drive.

items.question: Do you accept federal employee benefit plans?
items.answer: Yes. As an AMQ-registered massage therapist, Olha provides official receipts accepted by most federal employee benefit programs, including PSHCP and equivalent plans. No doctor's referral is required — check your plan for covered amounts and eligible practitioners.

aylmer.title: Massage in Aylmer, Gatineau
aylmer.subtitle: Professional massage therapy for Aylmer residents — a short drive east along Boulevard des Allumettières to Hull–Gatineau.
paragraphs: Aylmer is Gatineau's western residential community — a mix of families, outdoor enthusiasts, and professionals who value the quieter, greener setting along the Ottawa River. Whether you are recovering from a hike in Parc de la Gatineau, managing the physical demands of a desk job, or looking for a regular wellness routine, professional massage therapy is available at Olha Shelest's practice in Hull — just 15 to 20 minutes east along Boulevard des Allumettières.

Many Aylmer residents already travel this route daily for work, shopping, and services. Booking a massage session on the way home from work, or combining it with other errands in Hull, makes it easy to maintain a regular self-care practice.

Olha offers six massage types tailored to different needs: therapeutic massage for pain and injury, deep tissue for chronic tension, relaxation massage, lymphatic drainage, children's massage (with parental presence), and couples massage. AMQ-registered with official insurance receipts provided for all sessions.

gettingHere.title: Getting Here from Aylmer
gettingHere.body: From Aylmer, take Boulevard des Allumettières east through Gatineau toward Hull. The practice is at 148 Rue Eddy Unit 2 in Hull, approximately 15 to 20 minutes from central Aylmer depending on traffic. Free street parking is available on Rue Eddy and nearby streets.

items.question: How far is your practice from Aylmer?
items.answer: The practice is at 148 Rue Eddy in Hull — approximately 15 to 20 minutes east of central Aylmer by car along Boulevard des Allumettières. It is a straightforward drive with no highway required.

items.question: Do you offer services suited for active families in the Aylmer area?
items.answer: Yes. Children's massage (with parental consent and presence), sports recovery massage, and therapeutic massage are all available. Many clients from Aylmer come for recovery after outdoor activities in Parc de la Gatineau or Parc Brébeuf, and for managing the physical demands of family life.

items.question: Is there parking when coming from Aylmer?
items.answer: Yes — free street parking is available on Rue Eddy and surrounding streets. There is no parking fee and spaces are typically available during business hours.

outaouais.title: Massage Therapy in the Outaouais
outaouais.subtitle: Serving the entire Outaouais region — from Hull and Aylmer to Chelsea, Wakefield, and beyond — with professional, AMQ-registered massage therapy.
paragraphs: Olha Shelest offers professional massage therapy from her practice in Hull–Gatineau, at the centre of the Outaouais region. Whether you are in Hull, Aylmer, the Gatineau sector, Buckingham, Masson-Angers, or in the surrounding communities of Chelsea, Cantley, or Wakefield, her practice is a convenient destination for therapeutic and wellness massage.

The Outaouais region spans a diverse area — from urban Hull and Aylmer along the Ottawa River to the forested hills of the MRC des Collines-de-l'Outaouais. Residents of the region share common needs: relief from desk work and commute fatigue, recovery from outdoor activities like skiing and hiking in Parc de la Gatineau, and the ongoing maintenance of physical and mental wellbeing.
As an AMQ (Association des Massothérapeutes du Québec) member, Olha provides official receipts accepted by most Quebec group insurance plans and federal employee benefit programs.

gettingHere.title: Getting Here
gettingHere.body: The practice is centrally located at 148 Rue Eddy Unit 2 in Hull–Gatineau — accessible from all parts of the Outaouais. From central Gatineau: 10–15 minutes. From Aylmer: 15–20 minutes. From Chelsea: 15 minutes. From Buckingham: approximately 45 minutes. Free street parking is available on Rue Eddy.

items.question: Do you serve clients from across the Outaouais region?
items.answer: Yes. Clients come from all parts of the Outaouais — Hull, Aylmer, the Gatineau sector, Buckingham, and surrounding communities including Chelsea, Cantley, and Wakefield. The practice is centrally located in Hull at 148 Rue Eddy, accessible from all directions.

items.question: Are massage receipts covered by Quebec insurance plans?
items.answer: Yes. As an AMQ-registered massage therapist, official receipts are provided for all sessions. These are accepted by most Quebec group insurance plans and federal employee benefit programs. No doctor's referral is required — check your specific plan for coverage details.

items.question: Do you offer massage for sports and outdoor activity recovery?
items.answer: Yes — therapeutic and deep tissue massage are well suited for recovery from skiing, hiking, cycling, and other activities popular in the Outaouais region. Massage helps reduce muscle soreness, improve range of motion, and speed up recovery. Please mention your activity and any areas of concern during intake.
